title: Monday, February 3rd
description: Burlington's feel-good news, events, weather and discussion all in one place. Every Monday, Wednesday and Friday.

title: Wednesday, February 5th
description: Burlington's feel-good news, events, weather and discussion all in one place. Every Monday, Wednesday and Friday.

title: Friday, February 7th
description: Burlington's feel-good news, events, weather and discussion all in one place. Every Monday, Wednesday and Friday.

title: Monday, February 10th
description: Burlington's feel-good news, events, weather and discussion all in one place. Every Monday, Wednesday and Friday.

title: Wednesday, February 12th!
description: Burlington's feel-good news, events, weather and discussion all in one place. Every Monday, Wednesday and Friday.

title: Friday, February 14th
description: Your Friday Fix: Snowstorms, Local Buzz & Weekend Plans – A winter storm is rolling in, but Burlington isn’t slowing down. From UVM’s big research milestone to weekend events, sports updates, and where to meet new people, here’s everything you need to know before you head into the long weekend.

title: Monday, February 17th
description: Your Monday Rundown: Wind Gusts, New Eats & Local Happenings – The cold isn’t letting up, but neither is Burlington. From icy ferry closures to Firehouse Subs’ grand opening, plus upcoming events, sports highlights, and where to warm up with live music, here’s what’s happening around town.

title: Wednesday, February 19th
description: Your Wednesday Rundown: Burlington’s still digging out, but that’s not stopping the fun. From record-breaking snowfall at Jay Peak to a Grammy winner heading our way, plus live music, comedy, and local news, here’s what’s happening.

title: Friday, February 21st
description: Your Weekend Rundown: Chilly Temps, Live Music & Big Wins – The cold holds firm, but so does Burlington’s energy. From warming trends on the horizon to UVM sports victories, upcoming concerts, and a local billion-dollar business sale, here’s what’s happening around town.

title: Monday, February 24th
description: Your Monday Rundown: Blustery Breezes, Big Wins & Burlington Buzz – The wind’s picking up, but so is the local action. From VP Vance’s Vermont ski trip to a million-dollar Powerball ticket, plus UVM playoff races, fresh events, and a new bill tackling road salt runoff, here’s what’s making waves around town.

title: Wednesday, February 26th
description: Wintry Mix, Local Buzz & Your Chance to Win! – Snow and slush are on the way, pothole crews are in action, and Burlington is buzzing with events. Plus, don’t miss our $50 giveaway for readers and the Burlington Scavenger Hunt—first to finish wins $30! Here’s what’s happening around town.

title: Friday, February 28th
description: Your Weekend Rundown: From slick roads and a shifting winter forecast to local news on a fresh rap direction, student calls for a gun ban, and downtown restaurant closures—plus busy beavers cleaning our waterways, sports wins, and a jam-packed weekend of events. Photo Scavenger Hunt still up to be won! Here's whats happening:

title: Monday, March 3rd
description: Chilly Sunshine, Maple Taps & a not-so-warm welcome to the VP - From protests and major water upgrades to Bernie’s “Fight Oligarchy” tour, Burlington’s news is in full swing. Sugarhouse season is tapping into our sweet side, and we’ve got the latest sports scores plus a midweek lineup of events to keep you busy. Get cozy, dive in, and discover what’s buzzing around the Queen City this week.

title: Wednesday, March 5th
description: Your Midweek Rundown: Burlington’s weather is on a roller coaster—from sudden chills and rising rivers to a promising drier weekend. Dive into the latest on UVM’s hiring freeze, Town Meeting Day political shifts, eco-friendly ice cream trucks, sports highlights, and an action-packed calendar of music gigs and community meetups.

title: Friday, March 7th
description: Your Weekend Rundown: Science Marches, Flower Show Fun, and Local Happenings – Burlington’s bustling with events this weekend! Support community protests, explore the vibrant Vermont Flower Show, enjoy live music, catch sports highlights, and discover the best local eats and things to do around town.

title: Monday, March 10th
description: Your Monday Rundown: Sun Past 6pm! Icy Breezes, Citizen Cider’s Big Move & UVM Playoff Thrills – Burlington’s chill tonight (with lows in the 20s) sets the stage for a busy week ahead. Discover how Citizen Cider is shifting gears to Flynn Ave, UVM’s breakthrough water quality AI and a chance to join Grace Potter on stage, plus tonight’s line dancing, tomorrow’s trivia, and sports highlights from UVM basketball, hockey, and lacrosse.

title: Monday, March 24th
description: We’ve been cooking up something special! And we’re hoping you will be too. We're calling it Btown Eats, come read about what it is! Plus here’s the Monday Rundown: Slushy Streets, New UVM President & More – From late-season snow showers to water-saving measures and a new face at the University of Vermont, and more.

title: Wednesday, March 26th
description: Your Midweek Brief: Leslie Jones, Jazz Nights & a Lingering Chill – Burlington welcomes comedy powerhouse Leslie Jones to the Flynn, while jazz nights keep the vibe warm across town. The spring chill isn't letting go just yet—expect cool temps, icy winds, and cozy reasons to stay indoors. Here's your quick news fix.

title: Monday, March 31st
description: Your Monday Rundown: Spring Temps, Main Street Mayhem & Robots Take Over UVM – A warm start to the week gives way to a chilly April Fool’s Day, with rain, snow, and sleet all on the menu. We’ve got updates on Burlington’s construction chaos, Winooski’s detour frustrations, a robotics showdown at UVM, and why your next Montreal trip just got slower. Plus: culinary showcases, jazz nights, dodgeball, and David Sedaris at The Flynn.

title: Wednesday, April 2nd
description: Wednesday’s Brief: Wintry Mix Tonight, 60s Tomorrow – Big Wins for Burlington Restaurants, Electric Planes, and What’s Up on Pine Street. Snow turns to rain tonight, but by Thursday we’re flirting with t-shirt weather. Inside: local spots land James Beard Award nods, Beta’s coast-to-coast electric flight touches down, and Pine Street braces for round two of construction. Plus, your stacked event lineup.

title: Friday, April 4th
description: Weekend Brief: Friday Sun, Weekend Rain & Civic Pride – Spring Market, Comedy, and a Fresh Look at BPD – Today’s sunshine gives way to a soggy weekend, but there’s no shortage of reasons to get out. We’ve got your guide to Burlington’s packed event lineup, from Friday night funk and indie rock to Sunday reggae and candlelit concerts. Plus: Vermont’s civic engagement gets a spotlight, a new report urges more participation, and Interim Chief Burke lays out his plan for the future of BPD. And don’t miss the Vermont Spring Market—your best bet for staying dry *and* supporting local.

title: Wednesday, April 9th
description: Tonight in BTV: Cold Snap, Comedy Jams & Council Shakeups – Burlington chills into the 20s, but the local scene’s heating up. Inside: Bernie’s $17 wage fight, a kitchen fire that displaced seven, and protests lighting up Church Street. Plus: new faces on the City Council, a missing person reward, live jazz, stand-up comedy, trivia night lineups, and what to expect from this weekend’s moody spring weather.

title: Friday, April 11th
description: Weekend Brief: Friday Clouds, Weekend Drizzles & Indoor Escapes – Violin Exports, Student Relief & Burlington's Indie Film Revival – Tonight’s overcast calm gives way to rain-soaked streets, but Burlington isn’t slowing down. From bass-heavy nights at Higher Ground to cozy screenings at The Film House, we’ve got your weekend mapped. Plus: a federal judge grants local students a lifeline, Vermont Violins goes global with sustainable strings, and Main Street’s indie cinema scene fills a long-empty void.

title: Tuesday, April 15th
description: Tuesday Brief (Plus New Sections): Thunder in the Forecast, Protests Downtown & Spring Skiing’s Last Stand – Scattered showers and scattered crowds define this week’s start. As Burlington cools off, the Tufts student deportation case heats up in federal court. Plus: ski lifts keep turning, salmon fill the lake, and UVM staff land a raise. Check out our new 101+ Summer Things-to-do in Burlington, this week's comic, and a new restaurant review: Pho Hong!

title: Friday, April 18th
description: Weekend Brief: Rain Rolls In, But Burlington Keeps Moving – Free Parking, Comedy Nights, and a Sunday 5K – Tonight’s clouds set the mood, but the city’s just getting started. From sketch improv to Repair Café drop-ins to, and a sunny window for Sunday’s free wellness run/walk at UVM, here’s what’s happening now—plus: fluoride debates, federal detentions, and a ski season that refuses to quit. Also check out our new 101+ Summer Things-to-do in Burlington, plus this week's comic and restaurant review: Pho Hong!

title: Monday, April 21st
description: Monday Brief: April Showers, Electric Ideas, and Midweek Music — Food Shelf Moves, Housing Gridlock, and a Jazz Festival Preview.  A damp start to the week gives way to dry skies and packed calendars. From the Electrify Vermont Summit and patio screenings to zoning shifts on Pine and a bold jazz lineup reveal, here’s what’s shaping Burlington right now.

title: Thursday, April 24th
description: Broadway at The Flynn, Illuminate Vermont Kicks Off, and Foam Brewers Turns 9 — Plus: Recycling Changes, Electric Planes, and Social Security Protests. Evan Hansen hits the stage tonight at the Flynn, South Burlington glows with music, food trucks and artisans lights starting Friday, and Foam’s weekend-long party brings music and special brews. Also in this edition: Burlington debates privatizing recycling, Beta adds all-electric planes to its fleet, and locals rally against Social Security cuts.

title: Monday, April 28th
description: Summer Temps, Quick Tuesday Storms & What to Catch Before the Clouds — New Affordable Housing, UVM’s Climate-Friendly Dining, and Joann Fabrics' Farewell. Burlington heats up with 75° skies before a quick thunderstorm Tuesday evening—perfect timing to squeeze in waterfront rides, community marches, and midweek shows. Inside: 65 new apartments open in Colchester, UVM rethinks campus dining with a local-first menu, City Council takes a stand on student rights, and crafters brace as Joann Fabrics shutters across Vermont. Your full rundown of what’s changing—and what’s worth showing up for this week.

title: Thursday, May 1st
description: Earth Day Coming Up, Warm Friday, Rainy Weekend & Derby Day at Switchback — SNAP Cuts, Student Release, and a Parks FarewellFriday brings a burst of warmth before showers return Saturday, just in time for Green Up Day, hobby horse races, and indoor jazz. Plus: a Columbia student walks free in Vermont, major cuts threaten food access, and Burlington’s parks department readies for new leadership.

title: Monday, May 5th
description: Weekday Brief: Rain returns to Burlington this week, with off-and-on showers and cooler temps through Thursday—but there’s no shortage of ways to stay engaged. From MJ Lenderman's show tonight to Champlain Consort’s free Renaissance performance, Latin dance parties at Switchback, and Whose Live Anyway? at The Flynn, the city’s staying busy. Meanwhile, UVM faces federal pressure over visa revocations and quietly scales back its DEI language, prompting concern across campus. Plus: a new state park access program opens the outdoors to low-income Vermonters, and emerald ash borers continue their slow destruction through Chittenden County’s tree canopy.

title: Thursday, May 8th
description: Mother's Day Weekend Brief: Rainy Start, Sunny Finish – Budget Cuts, Bouquet Builds & Burlington’s Music Landmark on Pause....A soaking Friday gives way to a brighter Sunday just in time for farmers markets, Saturday's Church St flower giveaways, and Mahler’s Titan. Plus: Nectar’s shutters for summer, city hall faces hard choices, and Foam taps a mimosa brew for Make-A-Wish.

title: Monday, May 12th
description: Monday Brief: Brisk Tonight, Then Summer Warmth! – Downtown \"Crisis\" & City Cuts; VTSU Up, Overdose Prevention Center Debated – Warm, sunny days lead into a bustling Burlington week. From Queen City's \"Knot Knite\" and Four Quarters' Bluegrass BBQ to Vermont Green FC's kickoff bash, your social schedule's covered. Plus: Over 100 businesses demand downtown solutions, city layoffs hit amid budget woes, and we dish on whether Shanty on the Shore still reels you in.

title: Thursday, May 15th
description: Weekend Brief: Warm Night, Wetter Weekend! – Vermont Green FC kicks off in tonight's warmth, then prep for stormy skies. We're covering CEDO's new lead, UVM's grad weekend, and the city's big student move out project, plus where to find art, community repairs, and fresh tacos amidst the shifting weather.

title: Monday, May 19th
description: Tonight's cool, cloudy skies bring a chance of evening rain, setting a temperate tone for a week filled with community connection and important city discussions. We're diving into City Council's detailed strategies for revitalizing downtown, COTS's promising expansion plans for its Pearl Street shelter, and how you can join unique local events like a geologist led hike or a bookstagrammer meet up. Plus, get the scoop on the season's first Smugglers' Notch spectacle and how UVM is advancing cancer research right here at home, with Clinical Trials Awareness Day approaching.

title: Friday, May 23rd
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Gray Friday, Showery Saturday, Brighter Sunday Hopes – Plex Fest's ONE Takeover, E-Bike Decisions & Meal Program Tensions – Tonight's cool, gray skies lead into a dynamic weekend: brave the showers for the Old North End's unmissable Plex Arts Fest or the downtown Mini Golf adventure. Plus, get the inside scoop on the contentious Food Not Bombs relocation and the future of Burlington's e-bike scene, before Sunday's Marathon brings a brighter outlook.

title: Monday, May 26th (Memorial Day)
description: Your Early Week Boost: Sun's Out, Warmth Building Through Midweek – Top Gun Night, Jumpstart Expo & Marathon Memories – Enjoy today's lovely sunshine carrying us into a warm Tuesday and Wednesday, perfect for Switchback's outdoor movie or Generator's innovation showcase. We're also looking back at the marathon's energy, noting a foodie farewell in the ONE, and parsing the latest from Winooski schools before showers make their return.

title: Thursday, May 29th
description: Tonight's Briefing: Cloudy Eve, Brighter Friday, Rainy Weekend Kickoff & Sunnier Sunday Hopes – Pride Gala Shines, Tech Hub Funds Pulled & ArtsRiot's Farewell – Celebrate a community milestone tonight. As the weekend brings both a massive waterfront celebration and a damp forecast, get the scoop on shifts for Vermont's tech scene, the South End's cultural landscape, and who's now behind the counter at an Old North End institution.

title: Monday, June 2nd
description: Your Evening Briefing: Brace for summer heat (and Thursday thunder)! Navigate the Jazz Fest wave, grab local bounty at the ONE Market, dissect the Mayor's budget plan & the divisive downtown letter storm, try a new Church Street stroll, and mourn (or just note) a quick venue closure. Plus, big time City Council meeting this Monday; check out the scoop!

title: Thursday, June 5th (Jazz Fest this Week!)
description: Your Jazz Fest Game Plan: A Stormy Start, A Funk-Filled Weekend, $16B GlobalFoundries Investment (~$120 million for Essex facility) & A City Hall Shakeup – Brace for some Thursday thunderstorms before diving into a massive Jazz Fest weekend. We’ll guide you to the can't-miss free shows on the waterfront, mark the bittersweet final nights at Nectar’s, and break down the Mayor's major government overhaul and what that big chip investment really means for Vermont.

title: Thursday, June 12th
description: A Weekend of Haze & Hops: As the smoke finally clears, get ready for a packed weekend featuring the Loving Day celebration, a big beer release on the waterfront, and two great farmers markets. We're also breaking down the ongoing short-term rental fight and the Governor's veto that leaves the state's emergency housing program in limbo.

title: Monday, June 16th
description: Your Monday Briefing: Juneteenth Streetball Hoops & Music, A 'Canada Street' Rename on Church St?, & South End Closures – Get ready for a week of celebrations with Juneteenth events kicking off from South Burlington to downtown. Get the details on the South Burlington pedestrian bridge finally moving ahead and a bold new proposal for the Pine Street Barge Canal area. Meanwhile, City Hall debates a Canadian rebrand for Church Street and South End drivers should beware of parkway construction, all under increasingly warm and humid skies.

title: Thursday, June 19th
description: Holy Smokes, It's a Packed Weekend – From today's storms to a perfect Friday, navigate the city's biggest celebrations. We've got the scoop on the weekend's best events, from block parties to park concerts. Plus, the scoop on Shy Guy Gelato's sale and a surprise $1.8M budget hole.

title: Monday, June 23rd
description: Btown's Heat Wave Edition: Sweltering Start, Midweek Storms, Cooler Relief – Gardener's Supply's Future, First BtownBrief Meet-Up Event & A Packed Week of Events – Find out where to beat the record heat as cooling centers open, then get the scoop on tonight's City Council meeting covering downtown traffic and City Place development. Plus, we're looking ahead to a week of big music with Waxahatchee and Peach Pit, a major groundbreaking at the Shelburne Museum, and how a new study proves Vermont's plastic bag ban is working.

title: Thursday, June 26th
description: Your Weekend Kickstart: Cloudy Thursday, Rainy Friday, Drier Sunday Ahead – Main Street's Nightly Reopening, A New Art House & A Downsized Festival of Fools – From tonight's friends mixer and fire show to a soggy Saturday, your plans await. Get the details on Main Street's traffic compromise, the city's tough new budget, the grassroots effort bringing a cinema back to downtown, and how a beloved festival was saved by local businesses for this year.

title: Monday, June 30th (4th of July Edition!)
description: Your Week Ahead: Hot Start, Stormy Middle, Fireworks Gamble – BTV's July 3rd Blowout, Dead's New Digs & Airport Upgrades – Today's heat gives way to midweek storms, putting Thursday's massive waterfront fireworks display in question. Find out where to catch the new Grateful Dead 'residency', what $5.8M means for the airport, and all the details for the biggest party of the summer before things (hopefully) clear up for the weekend.

title: Thursday, July 3rd (July 4th Weekend Edition)
description: It's BUSY this Weekend: Heavy Clouds Thursday, Perfect Fourth, Sunny & Hot Weekend – Waterfront Fireworks, Odd Fest Vibes & a Nectar's Cameo – Tonight's storms clear out just in time for the fireworks, leading into a sun-drenched holiday weekend perfect for Odd Fest's offbeat bash. Plus, get the inside scoop on the city's scrapped overnight parking plan, the state's massive education overhaul, and new strains on the employee-owned business model.

title: Monday, July 7th
description: Your Week Ahead: Stormy Start, Cooler Break & Midweek Heat – Trivia Showdowns, Champlain's Shakeup & a Party on the Bricks – Navigate tonight's thunderstorms toward a cool Tuesday perfect for a trivia dilemma (The Moth or Taylor Swift?). Then, get ready for the return of Party on the Bricks, plus the story on Champlain College's academic overhaul and the ongoing meal service relocation drama.

title: Thursday, July 10th
description: Your Hot Weekend Brief: Sun & Heat Take Over, Stray Storms Possible – Summervale's Return, Howard Center's Future & a Sobering Anniversary – Kick off the weekend with tonight's Party on the Bricks, then plan your sun-soaked Saturday around the huge one-day Summervale festival. Plus, get the inside story on the critical cuts facing our largest social service provider, the pivot by a beloved local food company, and why this specific date holds such a heavy meaning for Vermonters.

title: Monday, July 14th ('Our Next Heatwave!' Edition)
description: Your Midweek Heat Wave Primer: Stormy Monday, Sizzling 90s To Follow – VT Green's dramatic title win, a generational wastewater upgrade & an uncertain future for Afghan Vermonters – Plus, celebrate a new 15,000-gallon aquarium at ECHO, get ready for the next Party on the Bricks, and navigate a major bike path closure.

title: Thursday, July 17th (Lots to Do this Weekend!)
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: A Hero's ESPY, Infrastructure Woes & Border Stories – Once tonight's thunderstorms pass, a stunningly perfect Saturday unfolds for the Vermont Brewers Fest, the Antique Boat Show, or celebrating the Green's playoff berth. Get the full story on Burlington's own Ilona Maher taking home a national award, the financial crisis threatening GMT's future, the wastewater leak that just closed our beaches, and the complex local stories unfolding at the Canadian border.

title: Monday, July 21st (Two New Ideas to Introduce!)
description: Your Monday Evening Roundup: Perfect Running Weather Tonight, Heat Wave Thursday – Cars & Foam Waterfront Launch, OGE's 30-Year Block Party Plans & Teen Governor Dreams – Tonight's 70s bliss turns steamy by midweek: catch the lakeside trail runs or ONE Market before humidity returns. Plus, Vermont Green FC's comeback magic continues, public media faces deep cuts, and why Burlington baseball might be better without the big leagues, all before the food truck invasion hits Pine Street. Be sure check out the new Burlington Table idea, and also the teaser for the weekly Puzzle Hunts!

title: Thursday, July 24th (Must Read!)
description: Your Weekend Guide: Triple H Heatwave, Friday Storms, Smoky Saturday – The Ramble's Massive Return, VT Green's Historic Final & The Pit's Rebirth – Tonight's sweltering heat kicks off a packed weekend: brave potential storms for Vermont Green's sold-out final, then dive into the Old North End's beloved Ramble or OGE's 30th Anniversary bash. Plus, get the scoop on Jordan Klepper's \"Rue Canada\" visit, the troubling detention of Winooski's superintendent, and the long-awaited transformation of the downtown \"Pit.\" And don't forget to sign up for Burlington Table! As well as trying your hand at the new Queen City Puzzle, get your name on the leaderboard!

title: Monday, July 28th
description: Your Monday Matchup: Soupy Start, Swampy Week, September-Like Relief Coming. Fools Block Party Returns, UVM's New President Speaks & Police Chief's Prison Pivot. Tonight's foggy 90-degree sauna gives way to wildfire haze before Thursday's fall preview. Plus, the inside story on how 30+ businesses saved Festival of Fools, Vermont Green FC's championship run continues, and why Canadians are boycotting our border towns, all while Burlington rallies through construction chaos and budget cuts.

title: Friday, August 1st (Festival of Fools Weekend)
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: September-Style Sunshine Returns to Burlington – Fools Block Party Saves the Day, Nectar's Final Curtain & Dragon Boats on the Lake – Tonight's crisp 50s drop gives way to a flawless 80-degree weekend: catch rescue dogs with world records and aerial acrobatics as Burlington rallies to resurrect Festival of Fools. Plus, the inside story on El Gato's employee takeover, why Nectar's chains came off for good, and King Street's urgent call for male mentors, before Sunday's dragon boat races paddle for a cause.

title: Monday, August 4th
description: Your Monday Momentum: Smoky Skies Clear Tuesday, Weekend Heats Up – Vermont Green's Championship Glory, Church Street's 45th Sidewalk Sale Bonanza & Gardener's Supply Shocker – Tonight's hazy conditions give way to a bustling week: score deals and catch live music during five days of sidewalk shopping, witness Mumford & Sons with Noah Kahan Thursday, or debate ICE tactics at tonight's City Council. Plus, the inside story on our soccer champs, traffic headaches on Pine Street, and why 40 Gardener's workers are suddenly job hunting.

title: Monday, August 11th
description: Your Monday Evening Update: Heat Wave Continues, Wednesday Relief Coming – Meditation Tonight, Moth StorySLAM Tomorrow & Music All Week – Burlington sizzles through another 90+ degree day while downtown construction offers weekend reprieve. Plus, the inside story on Nectar's demise, $62.5M solar funding cut impacts low-income Vermonters, boaters scramble after Charlotte marina closure, and why Vermont Green FC fans raised $25K during their championship run. Cool off with tonight's transformative meditation or Emma Cook at Radio Bean before Thursday's packed lineup at Shelburne Vineyard, Foam, and Flicks in the Park.

title: Friday, August 15th (Packed Weekend)
description: Friday Morning Dispatch: Pleasant 80s today, Sizzling Saturday, Stormy Sunday, Urban Rangers Navigate 'Hurricane' of Camps – Cider Donuts Launch, Mozartiana Opens & 60th Anniversary Celebrations – After weeks of heat, Burlington gets a break, especially next week. Plus, inside the impossible job of managing 550 acres with two rangers, What Ales You emerges from its basement after 54 years, and why Building Burlington's Future raised $1.3M to turn resident frustration into policy wins.

title: Monday, August 18th
description: Monday Evening Dispatch: Fall Preview Chill, Wednesday Showers Coming – Dodgeball, Comedy & Vinyl Nights, Summer's Final Party on the Bricks – Tonight's 60s cooldown kicks off a week of stellar events: unleash frustrations at pickup dodgeball, catch Nimesh Patel's standup, or groove to nine-piece High Summer before Thursday's season finale. Plus, Main Street's weekend reprieve for struggling businesses, Scott's double rejection of Trump's Guard requests, and why 212 salmon signal hope despite PFAS fears

title: Friday, August 22nd (Champlain Valley Fair Kicks Off)
description: Your Weekend Lineup: Sunny 80s Today, Drought Relief Sunday Maybe – First African Landing Day's Mural Creation, Recovery Rave & Monster Trucks – Tonight's live music triple-header (Billy Wylder, 38 Special, Spring Chickens) kicks off a packed weekend: witness community art-making at Roosevelt Park, dance for recovery at City Hall, or catch the Farmers Market harvest. Plus, the inside story on Chris Harper's mysterious disappearance, Burlington's business crisis deepens as the Governor weighs in, and why People's Kitchen is serving protest with their free meals

title: Monday, August 25th
description: Your Monday Evening Dispatch: Cool Comfort Through Midweek, Fair Weather for Festival Fun – City Council's Safety Showdown, Pearl Street Bomb Scare Aftermath & UVM's Growing First-Gen Wave – Tonight's council tackles City Hall Park crackdowns while singing circles and dodgeball offer lighter alternatives. Navigate Tuesday's packed slate from treasure hunt bike rides to aerial silks, Wednesday's $99 carload fair deal, Thursday's demolition derby madness, plus inside details on Perkins' explosive devices, Tromp's $575k presidency, and why 80% of Vermont's college-bound chose UVM this year.

title: Monday, September 1st (Labor Day!)
description: Your Labor Day Evening Update: Clouds Rolling In, Rain Coming Thursday – Dodgeball Tonight, Disney Karaoke & Park Planning This Week – As summer unofficially ends, Burlington grapples with public safety tensions: the mayor and governor trade barbs over City Hall Park enforcement while BED faces unprecedented audit scrutiny. Plus, viral Vermont kindness, South Burlington's bus driver comeback, and why your trees are dropping October leaves in September

title: Friday, September 5th (Art Hop Weekend)
description: Friday's Storms Give Way to Art Hop Launch, Saturday's Rain Can't Stop the Party – Vermont's EV Charging Victory, Lake Champlain Historic Lows & Farmers Hauling Water Daily – Last night's thunderstorms clear for BCA's 270-seat unveiling and dance parties, before Saturday's soaking brings indoor glassblowing demos and Live from BCA, while dairy farms face $100K drought bills, a Shelburne cop dodges prison, and Sunday's perfect weather awaits the Earth Charter celebration.

title: Monday, September 8th
description: Monday Evening Dispatch: Frost Advisory Tonight, 70s Ahead – Council Tackles $2.2M Overdose Center, Police Contract Boost & Nudity Ban – From Dinosaur Cookies to Grief Workshops, Spanish Meetups to Memorial Unveilings – Your guide through Burlington's biggest week yet, as The Gryphon closes, wake sports face bans, and St. Joseph's survivors find healing in Kieslich Park while F-35s prep for two weeks of night flights

title: Friday, September 12th
description: Your Weekend Lineup: Foggy Friday Clears for Grace Potter's Symphony Show, Saturday's Possible Scattered Showers & Sunday's Sunshine – Grand Point North Kicks Off, Brain Freezer 5K Ice Cream Challenge, Dancing with the Stars for Charity – From Burlington Square's luxury opening amid downtown struggles to South Burlington's phone-free classrooms, plus the Council's OPC grant approval and Simple Roots' bittersweet farewell after 11 years.

title: Monday, September 15th (Oktoberfest coming soon!)
description: Your Monday Evening Brief: Summer's Last Stand at 85° Before Weekend Frost – Kava Circles, Spanish Meetups & Gaffigan's Hot Pockets – The fog lifted this morning to reveal three more days of 80s warmth, Burlington gathers for everything from Traditional Tuesday's communal kava bowl to Thursday's glow-in-the-dark pickleball, while Main Street businesses wonder what will survive another year of jackhammers. Plus, the Ticonderoga's 70-year landlocked anniversary, Ridin' High's basement dwelling demise, and why UVM Medical Center just lost $88 million for subsidizing New York hospitals with Vermont insurance rates.

title: Friday, September 19th
description: Fall Chill Weekend Ahead: Oktoberfest Returns to Pine Street, Jim Gaffigan at The Flynn & Prison Overcrowding Crisis – Clear skies and 60s beckon for tonight's packed lineup from comedy royalty to punk at Foam, while Saturday's frost warning can't dampen Giant Pumpkins in Colchester, Latin beats at City Hall Park, or Essex Junction's car-free Main Street party. Plus, deep dives on Vermont's $789M pandemic housing investment, UVM Health Network CEO's sudden exit, and why Tracy Drive residents keep falling into mysterious 70-year-old holes

title: Friday, September 26th
description: Weekend Awaits: Rain Wrapping Up for Sunshine Spectacular – Burlington Square Finally Opens, Extreme Drought Spreads & Housing Wars Heat Up – Tonight's departing drizzle sets up perfect conditions for Old Spokes Home's Fall Fundo, Vermont PoutineFest's gravy-soaked showdown, and Mission Murals' community paint days transforming Main Street's biggest eyesore. Meanwhile, Kimmel's still off local airwaves, Beta eyes Wall Street, and Burlington's overdose center faces more delays as Progressives unveil bold housing moves.

title: Monday, Sept 29th
description: Your Monday Evening Update: 80° Today, Frost Thursday Morning, 30s Wake-Up Call – Silent Films & City Council Tonight, NASA's Newest Vermont Astronaut & Drought Crisis Deepens – Berlin's running fire hoses to wells, Shelburne celebrates 94 affordable homes, while volunteers paint over overnight vandalism: catch free 1916 cinema at Burlington Beer Co, weigh in on public indecency laws at City Hall, then plan for tomorrow's Women Business Conference, ecstatic dance sessions, and Wednesday's Oktoberfest before Thursday's blues legend Kingfish takes Higher Ground.

title: Friday, October 3rd
description: Your Summer Encore Weekend: Record-Breaking 70s/80s & Peak Foliage – Construction Fest Takes Over Church St., The Gryphon’s Full Story & City Hall Shake-Ups – This weekend's record-breaking warmth is your perfect excuse to check out Construction Fest on Church Street, a 1920s swing dance, or the Vermont Anime and Gaming Expo. Plus, get the full, complicated story behind The Gryphon’s sudden closure, a major staff shake-up in the Mayor's office, and the City Council's controversial vote to ban public nudity, all inside the newsletter.

title: Monday, October 6th
description: Monday's Summer Encore & Your Week Ahead: Record 80s Today, Rain Relief Tomorrow, Reality Check Wednesday – Council Tackles Tax Fairness & Financial Disclosures Tonight, ICE's Williston Surveillance Hub Exposed, Plus Dodgeball Therapy & Amy Grant to Emo Night – Your guide through Burlington's October heatwave surprise, from today's civic showdowns and Tuesday's packed cultural calendar (Banned Books! Cycling Films! The Moth!) to the weekend's festival of denial that autumn exists.

title: Friday, October 10th
description: Friday Fall Forecast: Sunshine & 60s All Weekend – Margaret Cho Tonight, Zombie Walk Returns, Pride Center Crisis – Plus ski swaps closing Sunday, Supreme Court Justice's secret UVM visit revealed, Nash Barlow's hometown dream realized, graffiti wars escalate, food shelf serves 4,000+, Howard Center cuts harm reduction, and where to catch Saturday's perfect weather at Essex's Meet Me On Main and Shelburne orchards.

title: Monday, October 13th (Chilly, Fall Days)
description: Your Monday Momentum: Rainy Start, Clearing Skies Ahead – Gov's Crime Court 'Surge', ICE Protest Draws 100+, Citizen Cider's Comeback Gamble – Tonight's steady showers set the stage for a week of Burlington shifts: from The Very Hungry Caterpillar's puppet magic to line dancing at Isham Farms, plus South Burlington's surprise ranking as Hispanic entrepreneur haven. Get the inside story on Essex Junction's Main Street battle, the unanimous rejection of Vermont's new women's prison, and why half our maple syrup isn't making the grade, before Thursday brings ballet, Friends trivia, and ECHO's adults-only fear fest.

title: Friday, October 17th (VT International Film Festival Starts!)
description: Your Weekend Forecast: Sunny 60s Saturday, Warmer 70s Sunday – No Kings Protests Take Burlington, Halloween Howl Hits Oakledge & Beta's Billion-Dollar Debut – Tonight's Mystical Market under the stars kicks off a packed weekend: join citywide marches or catch The Strictly Hip's Tragically good tribute. Plus, the inside story on Sen. Douglass facing resignation calls, Vermont's worries about federal reimbursements during the shutdown, and how volunteers are tackling downtown's trash crisis before Sunday's bike lane revolution begins.

title: Monday, October 20th
description: Monday's Rainy Reality Check: Scattered Showers Through Tonight, Tuesday's Brief Sunshine – 'No Kings' Protests Pack City Hall, Syringe Returns Hit 90% Goal, Pride Center Hotline Lives On – Tonight's soaking sets up a week of weather whiplash while Burlington grapples with 427,774 missing needles, Vorsteveld Farm faces contamination lawsuit, and UVM's $2,029 apartments sit empty. Plus, catch the dating game show disrupting Tinder Wednesday, or go get pointers for prepping your car for winter.

title: Friday, October 24th (Halloween Weekend Edition)
description: Your Friday Evening Outlook: Clouds Linger Through Halloween Weekend Madness – Scott's Safety Plan, Food Stamp Crisis & Sotomayor Inspires. Tonight's stubborn showers give way to a packed Halloween weekend of punk shows, dog costume contests, and Tech Jam networking. Burlington digests the governor's 14-point safety initiative while Vermont scrambles to backstop threatened food benefits, plus Justice Sotomayor's historic UVM visit urges students to stay engaged despite setback

title: Monday, October 26th
description: Monday's Mix: Cool Clouds Today, Sunshine Returns Tomorrow – Tina Turner Takes The Flynn, Murder Ballads at Foam & Micro-Cinema Rising – Tonight's packed slate includes mink-spotting bike rides, silent reading at Bar Renée, and tech policy town halls, while Tuesday brings roller derby boot camps and grotesque potluck dishes. Plus, Burlington's recycling crossroads, Scott's 14-point safety plan controversy, and why your Halloween costume should come from Vault Collective this year.

title: Wednesday, October 29th
description: Monday and Friday are for news & events. Wednesdays are for everything else worth highlighting. Come read the latest scoop!

title: Friday, October 31st (Halloween Edition!)
description: Your Halloween Weekend: A Spooky, Blustery Friday, Chilly Saturday, Brighter Sunday Hopes – Foam's Free MTV Bash, State vs. Feds on SNAP & COTS Shelter Opens – Tonight's wicked winds lead into a packed weekend: brave the chill for Foam's 'I Want My BTV' party or Saturday's Unlimited Pie Breakfast ($15). Plus, get the details on Vermont covering food stamps, the new BTV airport terminal, and the controversial blasting set for Wheeler Park, before Sunday's Mozart Requiem offers a classical finale, or, if you're feeling spicy, join in on the punk music video Sunday at Odd Fellows.

title: Monday, November 3rd
description: Monday Evening Dispatch: Showers Rolling Through, Council Decisions Ahead – Vermont's Insurance Crisis Hits 30,000 Families, 'Accountability Court' Targets 110 Repeat Offenders & School Choice Costs Exposed – Tonight's City Council confirms new Parks Director while rain dampens Burlington. Plus, families facing $40,000 premiums after subsidies expire, why Barstow taxpayers unknowingly fund ski academy tuitions, and South Burlington's e-bike crackdown despite zero accidents recorded.

title: Wednesday, November 5th
description: Your Mid-Week Guide: Burger Week's Wildest Flavors, The Great Bagel Debate, & Daily Food Deals – Local Rent Woes, Insurance Sticker Shock, & Holiday Volunteer Needs – This edition dives into the culinary scene: get your full guide to Burger Week's most extravagant and creative options. Plus, see the results of a passionate 120-comment war on the area's best bagel, find weekly drink deals, and see how you can help as a holiday elf.

title: Friday, November 7th
description: Your Weekend Forecast: Rain Tonight, Saturday's Golden Window, Sunday's Drizzle – Beta's Billion-Dollar Takeoff, Radio Bean's 25-Year Marathon & State Troopers Downtown – Navigate tonight's 40mph gusts before diving into 100+ bands at the Bean, the S.P.A.C.E Gallery's 'Small Works' opening reception, Tibet Festival's free admission, or Vermont Swings' anniversary dance. Plus, the inside story on UVM's moldy dorm rebellion and why Burlington's credit rating just hit a 16-year high, all before Tuesday's potential first valley snow.

title: Monday, November 10th
description: The First Snow Is Sticking: A Slick Commute Tonight, Cold Snap All Week, Mountain Peaks Get 4\" – Burton's Donna Carpenter, Free Vets Dinner, A 50th Anniversary Party & A 5-Course Beer Dinner – Tonight's rain-to-snow switch makes for a cozy (or slick) trip to the Thanksgiving Wine Tasting, Stranger Things trivia (on 11/11!), or the post-hardcore chaos of Emery. Plus, find ways to honor Veterans Day with a free meal or a talk on liberation, swap your winter clothes at Sandbox, and get the inside scoop on a crucial Ward 1 CDBG vote before the week wraps with ski films and reggae.

title: Wednesday, November 12th
description: Your Wednesday Briefing: First Snow Chaos, Late Fall Vibes, Wii Bowling for Good – Troopers Downtown, The Great Sandwich Poll & Syringe Program Stats – This week's icy, chaotic commute leads into a late-fall vibe: find out why State Troopers are patrolling Church Street and see Reddit's ranking of BTV's best sandwiches. Plus, get the staggering stats on the city's syringe program, discover the ONE's new hit pizza spot, and learn how you can play Wii Bowling for a cause.

title: Friday, November 14th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Quiet Friday, Wintry Mix Saturday Night, Sunday Snow – Goodbye Pine's Final Dance, Non-Fiction Comics Fest & Craft Fair Bonanza – Tonight's calm before the storm as freezing rain threatens Saturday evening travel. Plus, SNAP benefits finally arrive, downtown crime trending down, Vermont Comedy Club's nonprofit pivot, and 200+ preparing to winter outdoors without shelter options. And way more for this packed weekend.

title: Wednesday, November 19th
description: Your Wednesday Midweek Mix: Reddit's Best Lunch Spots Revealed, Howard Center's $17M Insurance Crisis & Tranq's Surge – \"Weird Al\" Hits the Expo, The Pinwheel's Essex Return & Winter Lights Tickets Selling Fast – Sail Beyond Cancer needs volunteers, Hula's Good Trade Makers Market just a couple weeks away, Green House Artists's open house coming up, and cozy winter walks await while mental health funding faces a perfect storm.

title: Friday, November 21st
description: Your Weekend Plans: Tonight's Theater & Auctions, Saturday's Market Madness, Sunday's Book Club Bliss – Act 76's Childcare Revolution Bears Fruit, CARES Mental Health Program Faces Uncertain Future & UVM Dorm Drama Gets Council Action – Navigate a packed weekend from Les Mis at Hunt Middle School to 100+ vendor craft fairs, while Burlington grapples with preserving mental health services and students demand livable housing. Plus, David Blow's lifetime achievement, Lois McClure's $20M education legacy, and why Strava is the new Tinder.

title: Monday, November 24th (Thanksgiving Week!)
description: Your Thanksgiving Week Kickoff: Clearing Skies Today, Mild & Rainy Wednesday, Dry Turkey Day – Big Heavy World's Sudden Closure, UVM Soccer's Overtime Heartbreak & Free Holiday Meals Citywide – Tonight's quiet start gives way to a packed week: Tuesday brings Stunt Nite and formal attire bike rides, Wednesday serves up Thanksgiving Eve comedy at Foam and live music across the county. Plus, get the scoop on why Vermont lawmakers are flying blind on outdated tech and where to run off your stuffing Thursday morning.

title: Wednesday, November 26
description: Your Midweek Mix: Podcast Deep Dives, Cozy Williston Lunch Spots & Holiday Prep – BTV Cares Gets Cut, Pride Center Saved by Mystery Donor – City Council's Traverse sounds off on downtown struggles and police staffing regrets while Garden of Eatin' tops the casual Williston lunch debate. Plus, the Church Street tree is up and ready for Friday's lighting, Oysterfest and a walkable Winooski market party are coming in December, and we've got plenty of reasons to embrace the cold before winter fully settles in.

title: Monday, December 1st
description: Snow Tomorrow, Cold Snap Friday, Layer Up – City Council Tonight, Education Tax Hikes & Pride Center's $350K Lifeline – Monday's sunshine fades fast as a coastal storm rolls in Tuesday morning with 2-4 inches for the Champlain Valley. Brave the flurries for Charlie Brown at The Flynn, Algernon Cadwallader at Higher Ground, or speed-pitch your way into a job at The Venetian. Plus, City Market's café closes again, a new movie theater rises from the ashes, and why your Queen City Park neighbors are fuming about stop signs.

description: Wednesday Deep Dive: Wales's Wellbeing Veto, $500K Tax Proposals & Vermont's \"Survival Guide\" – Btown Brief Merch Dropping Soon, Best Wings Showdown (Shelburne Tap House Takes the Crown), Fresh Snow Adventures from Oakledge to Lone Rock Point – Plus: Host an Exchange Student, NYE's 12-Hour Highlight Lineup & This Week's Dollar Wings

title: Friday, December 5th
description: Brrr-lington: Sub-Zero Start, Warmer Weekend Ahead – Church Street's Festive Friday Kickoff, Market Mania & Live Music Galore – Bundle up for tonight's frigid temps before Saturday's relative warmth brings The Good Trade, Generator's Holiday Market & High Summer's long-awaited debut at Foam. Plus: City Council recap: OPC Agreement, License Plate Readers, & Senior Support. BHS saves $6M on construction, school consolidation battle heats up, homeless shelters struggle to meet demand, and the secre

title: Monday, December 8th
description: Bitter Cold Through Tuesday, Snow Brewing for Wednesday – Flecktones at The Flynn, Black Cap's Union Fight & Closure, Belleville's Viral 'Proud of Me' Cakes – Wind chills hit minus 5 by morning, but the week ahead is packed: holiday trivia at Switchback, a gift swap at Oddfellows, Phish trivia at Higher Ground. Plus, the Whitneys' Church Street tree carries a deeper mission, and why Colchester has more mobile homes than anywhere in the county.

title: Wednesday, December 10th
description: Midweek Must-Reads: ICE Arrests Up Tenfold, Black Cap's Suspicious Shutdown Sparks 331 Comments – Reddit Crowns Hop Sing Best Chinese, North End Food Pantry Needs Drivers & Merch Dropping Friday – Vermont Edition unpacks how mass deportations threaten your grocery bill while locals debate whether union-busting killed Church Street's beloved coffee spot. Plus, score $1 drafts at Three Needs, catch Shakey Graves' two-night run, and discover why City Market's financial future has Burlington talking.

title: Friday, December 12th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Flurries & 20s Through Sunday, Warmer Days Ahead – Soccer Coach Shock, Rugby's Championship Run & Budget Gap Blues – Bundle up for a packed Saturday: reindeer at ECHO, Hadestown at The Flynn, Krampus marching Church Street, and the People's Skillshare across town. Sunday brings the Ugly Sweater Run and TubaChristmas. Plus, refugees lose food benefits, UVM's new president charms campus, and a South Burlington mutual aid group feeds neighbors on Market Street. Oh, and one more thing: the first ever Btown Brief merch drop is live today.

title: Monday, December 15th
description: Cold Monday, Big Warmup Coming – VT Air Guard Deploys to Puerto Rico, Beta's Billion-Dollar Expansion, Winter Lights & a Stacked Week Ahead – Bundle up tonight, but midweek temps hit the 40s before rain arrives. Plus: new Memorial Auditorium redevelopment plans revealed on worked on, license plate readers for traffic enforcement, Progressives play defense in March elections, and your guide to everything from Solstice Spins to Cookie Swaps to Shakey Graves at Higher Ground.

title: Friday, December 19th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Gusty Friday Gives Way to Quieter Skies – Record Snow Up High, Rugby Glory Down Low & Beta's Billion-Dollar Bet on Vermont – Tonight's wind and rain flip to snow by evening, so watch for icy spots. Church Street lights up for Festive Friday, the South End gets steamy at Kula's sauna party, and Saturday's packed with repair cafes, maker markets, and hot chocolate under the holiday lights. Plus: UVM rugby's national title run, Burlington's surprise road maintenance shakeup, a Head Start funding fight over three little words, and why 63 inches of powder on Mansfield is making history.

title: Monday, December 22nd
description: Snow Returns for a White Christmas, Holiday Rush Hits Church Street & the Mall – BlueCross Takes Aim at UVM Health Costs, Nine Dams Down & 125 Miles Reconnected – Tonight's flurries give way to Tuesday's real accumulation just in time to dust things up for the holidays. Catch Kizomba, dodgeball, or Spanish practice tonight before the snow rolls in, then explore the healthcare price war brewing between Vermont's biggest insurer and its biggest hospital. Plus: dam removals hit a record, Sandbox VT saves your favorite flannel, and where to find the best indie bookstore picks.

title: Friday, December 26th
description: Post-Holiday Chill: Teens Today, Snow South, Icy Monday Ahead – Reindeer at ECHO, Ugly Sweaters at Specs & Comedy Club Homecomings – Bundle up for a frigid Friday before Saturday's sunshine breaks through. Catch live reindeer, dance to Caribbean beats at Foam, or laugh with returning VT comedians. Plus, $18M in drought damage hits Vermont farms, Burlington's first emergency shelter opens, and Seven Days crowns the city's best bites of 2025. Be sure to take our new weekly Btown Brief Quiz to win prizes!

title: Monday, December 29th (New Year's Eve Edition!)
description: Your NYE Guide: Icy Start, Chilly Countdown, Single-Digit 2026 – Highlight Takes Over the Waterfront, Kwame Spins at Venetian & Budget Battles Ahead – Monday's slick roads give way to a packed New Year's Eve: catch Champ going up in flames, dance to vintage swing in 1920s attire, or ring it in queer and fierce at Higher Ground. Plus, the city's $10-12M deficit looms, Main Street bounces back, and a 95-year-old gets a fern named after her.

title: Friday, January 2nd
description: Your New Year Kickoff: Bitter Cold Settling In, Teens Through the Weekend – Sled Dogs at ECHO, Beethoven's Final Bow & a Glow Paint Party – Bundle up as temps plunge toward zero tonight, but don't let that stop you from catching Rose Asteroid at Foam or the Hash House Harriers at Oakledge. Plus, City Councilor Becca Brown McKnight gets real about Burlington's fiscal mess while the Abenaki curriculum controversy heats up in the Statehouse. Bundle up and read on.

description: Snow by Evening Commute, January Thaw Ahead – Venezuela Strikes Spark Church Street Protests, Green Mountain Boys Safe – Upper Pass Takes Over Simple Roots Space, Mad River Glen's $2.5M Land Push – Plus: Therapy Dog Buddy, a Mending Wall Quilt, Free Car Workshops & Your Week in Trivia Nights

title: Friday, January 9th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Warming Friday with Gusty Showers, Sloppy Sunday Mix – Accountability Court's Final Verdict, Church Street Closures & Charter Change Standoffs – Tonight's rain clears for a packed Saturday: catch Cuban salsa workshops, the Winter Farmers Market, or Back in Black at Higher Ground before the snow flies. Plus, Dear Lucy says goodbye, Democrats pick their council slate, and a retired professor turns pots and pans into art music.

title: Monday, January 12th
description: Monday Snow Showers, Midweek Warmup, Arctic Blast Ahead – Scott's Education Ultimatum, Last Place Economic Ranking & Shiffrin's Olympic Comeback – Shake off the January blues with City Council drama, bingo jackpots, and winter cycling tips tonight. Plus, why Vermont's housing crisis just got more attention, a student-led push for school board votes, and the I-89 pedestrian bridge finally moving forward.

title: Wednesday, January 14th
description: Your Midweek Mix: Kwame Collab Updates, Road Crisis Looms, Coziest Bars Ranked – New arts features incoming as The Btown Brief teams up with Burlington's beloved DJ, Senator White sounds the alarm on crumbling infrastructure and masked federal agents, plus Reddit crowns Farmhouse's basement the ultimate cozy spot. Catch Friday's snowsports auction, Saturday's cat show & repair café, or fuel up with $1 oysters and half-price burgers all week long.

title: Friday, January 16th
description: Snow Tonight, Arctic Blast Coming – Federal Probe Hits CVSD, Mayor's Housing Push & Free Healthcare in a Mosque – Light snow rolls in for Saturday before next week's dangerous cold; hit Skirack's Winter Swap, the Cat Show, or cozy up at the Murder Mystery Dinner. Plus, UVM dorms face inspection overhaul, a $33M transportation crisis looms, and Birnn Chocolates earns top honors after 110 years of Vermont truffles.

title: Monday, January 19th (MLK Day)
description: Your MLK Monday: Squalls Tonight, Deep Freeze Ahead – Free ECHO Admission, Gospel Choir Debuts & Mario Kart at the Museum – Today's flurries give way to potential whiteout conditions this evening before the coldest air of the season rolls in this weekend. Catch Seven Days' national TV moment, UVM's mushroom-infused burgers, and Beta's $10M buying spree – plus dodgeball, Dead yoga, and whether your lottery tickets are going digital.

title: Friday, January 23rd
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Snow Squalls Tonight, Dangerously Cold Saturday, Major Storm Sunday – Emergency Shelter Opens, Bernie's Healthcare Forum, James Beard Semifinalists & A Downtown Bar Says Goodbye – Bundle up as temps plunge to 20 below with wind chills hitting 40 below; catch Emil Wakim's SNL comedy, the Rock Point Center grand opening, or stay warm at the Winter Farmers Market. Plus, get the inside scoop on state land eyed for housing, Border Patrol's expanding reach, and why South Burlington pumped the brakes on joining the housing coalition.

title: Monday, January 26th
description: Snow Day Edition: 2-4 More Inches Tonight, Teens All Week – City Council's Apartheid Vote Round 3, Radio Bean's Future & Sweetwaters' Comeback – Bundle up as powdery snow blankets Burlington through the evening: catch tonight's packed Council agenda or dodge your civic duties at Miller Center dodgeball. Plus, Lee Anderson seeks new ownership for his 25-year-old venue, a Church Street classic returns by April, and Vermont USCIS workers get surprise Minnesota assignments.

title: Friday, January 30th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Bitter Cold Friday, Brighter Weekend Ahead – ICE Strike Solidarity, Equal Pay Victory & Population Woes – Bundle up for tonight's blue moon ski or reggae vibes at The Nine, then thaw out Saturday with free pancakes, Winter Carnivals, and Kathy Griffin's sharp wit. Plus, the inside story on a Burlington taxi driver's detention, a $475K settlement that echoes the #EQUALPAY moment, and why Vermont just posted the nation's worst population decline.

title: Monday, February 2nd
description: Your Monday Briefing: Sunshine & Low 20s Before Next Weekend's Arctic Blast – Heart Hunt Chocolate Rewards, Olympics Watch, Burlington's ICE Response & Bernie's Burlington Story – Grab a free chocolate bar on Church Street, catch Explosions in the Sky tonight, and prep for a packed week: Cirque Mechanics at The Flynn, a landmark book launch at Contois, moe.'s three-night run at Higher Ground. Plus, the latest on Vermont's REAL ID fee, a road salt shortage, GMT's frozen electric buses, and why your annual car inspection might become biennial.

title: Wednesday, February 4th
description: Your Midweek Highlight: Frigid Fun, Icy Plunges & Thawing Spirits – The Regulatory Wall, Pizza Crowns & Solo Dining Secrets – Kevin Chu breaks down why Vermont is losing its momentum and what it actually takes to keep young talent from hitting a dead end. We also reveal the community's favorite wood-fired pizza and the best local bars for a solo meal, plus your guide to \"smutty\" book fairs, the Snow Ball, and the Penguin Plunge; with enough $1 beers and $2 oysters to keep you cozy through the fr

title: Friday, February 6th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Snow Tonight, Arctic Blast Incoming, First Friday Everywhere – ICE Detentions Rock Burlington, Vermont's Ugliest House Gets a Glow-Up & a Self-Serve Ramen Bar Opens – Brave the flurries for Pine Street's twin art openings, Church Street's Heart Hunt finale and Snow Ball, or the Lakeside disco skate before temps nosedive. Saturday's Ren Faire and Valentine Market await the bold, Sunday's Super Bowl watch parties and Freezy Cheeks 5K defy the deep freeze. Plus, the Act 250 overhaul takes shape, Lake Champlain's salt problem gets real, and a Fletcher Free Library love story nearly 50 years in the making.

title: Monday, February 9th
description: Bitter Cold Fading, Snow Rolling In Tuesday – Knot Knite for AALV, Moth StorySLAM's Love Hurts, Stavros at the Flynn – Bundle up one last time as wind chills bottom out, then brace for midweek flurries. Catch the ICE preparedness surge reshaping Burlington's response, $8.4M in housing loans jumpstarting the South End, and Beta's expanding footprint pushing out small shops. Plus: Vermont's junk food ban debate, a 19-year-old shooting pros for Sports Illustrated, and a packed Valentine's week lineup from Radio Bean jams to bouquet building.

title: Wednesday, February 11th
description: Midweek Deep Cuts: DJ Fattie B's Hospital Bed Epiphany & the Card Game Born From It – SBA's $83M in Vermont Loans You Might Be Missing – Reddit's Cheap Eats Rankings Are In (Morning Light Ties UVM's Cafeteria?) – A Photographer Fell Through Lake Champlain Ice – Olympians Cochran-Siegle & Moltzan Bring Home Hardware – Plus, Pie & Ice Cream at Sisters of Anarchy, Valentine's orchestral date night, Mardi Gras at Hotel Vermont, and the full weekly podcast roundup from Vermont Edition to Vermont Comedy Club territory.

title: Friday, February 13th
description: Your Valentine's Weekend Kickoff: Cold Friday, Warming Trend, February Thaw Loading – Flash Tattoos for a Cause, Candlelit Quartets & a 50-Year Olympic Drought Broken – Pine Street is the place to be this weekend between the Farmers Market, SEABA's art market and AO Glass, while the Flynn stacks up the VSO, Fortune Feimster and a Princess Bride screening. Plus, 'Downtown Lights' aims to save winter retail, the Afghan Alliance gets new leadership amid ICE fears, and Vermont moves to regulate AI deepfakes before Election Day

title: Monday, February 16th (President's Day)
description: Monday Flurry, Tuesday Thaw, Wednesday Wild Card – Mardi Gras on Cherry Street, Pinball Throwdowns & A Packed Midweek Lineup – Presidents' Day eases into a mild Tuesday with Cajun music, trivia at Queen City Brewery and bike tales at Old Spokes Home. Meanwhile, Smuggs sells after 29 years, a Church Street boutique gets a second life, the OPC site search hits a wall, and students across Chittenden County walk out over ICE.

title: Friday, February 20th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Heavy Snow Tonight, Cloudy Saturday, Sunday's the Pick – Shiffrin's Gold, Tariffs Toppled & Schools in Crisis – Brave the snow for Burton's $10K Winter Jam at Champlain College, then dig out tomorrow for this weekend's Winterlude's fire pits, the Drag Ball at Higher Ground, or Abstract VT's first-ever live podcast recording. Plus, a Burlington company helped topple Trump's tariffs at the Supreme Court, Beta's board shakeup hits close to home, and Vermont's vanishing students are forcing tough questions about which schools survive.

title: Monday, February 23rd
description: Your Monday Evening Wrap: Deep Freeze Tonight, Clipper Snow Wednesday – Baruth's Surprise Exit, Council Power Plays & Vermont's Youth Exodus – Bundle up as wind chills hit zero, then dive into the Senate shakeup, $30M in housing investments, the fight over UVM's Grounds Crew, and why knitters across Chittenden County are reaching for red yarn. Plus, Tuesday's packed lineup from bike rides to Kinky Boots.

title: Wednesday, February 25th
description: Your Mid-Week Catch-Up: Mayoral Truths, Donut Debates, Ice Bar Dreams – ICE Emergency Plans, Top Italian Eats & Hobbit Bar Hunts – Dive into a candid podcast unpacking the Mayor's take on Burlington's unsheltered crisis and federal immigration fears. Plus, settle the score on the city's best pastries and pasta, package 3D-printed whistles for community safety, and find out where locals go to drink in the dark, before this weekend's Ice Bar and The Black Experience bring the city to life.

title: Friday, February 27th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Gusty Friday, Spring-Like Saturday, Arctic Sunday Snap – Ice Bar Hoedowns, Dr. Kendi at the Flynn & BETA's Billion-Dollar Bet – Tonight's south wind ushers in a wild weekend swing: catch Maddy Smith or the Palaver Strings before Saturday's 40-degree warmth brings out the Farmers Market crowds and Rokeby's free museum day. Plus, Town Meeting TV preps for its charmingly scrappy election night, a Church Street kiosk seeks its next act, and maple farmers race to adapt before the season slips away. And an incredible UVM's mens basketball breakdown from their Thursday game.

title: Monday, March 2nd
description: Your Monday Morning Briefing: One Last Subzero Night, Then March Warms Up – Town Meeting Day Packed Calendar, Purim Celebrations & Brit Floyd's Laser Spectacle – Bundle up tonight but say goodbye to winter's grip as 50 degree temps loom by the weekend. Tomorrow brings Election Day bake sales, waterfront 5Ks, a full moon dance party and the Flynn lit up Pink Floyd style. Plus, artifacts from Burlington's very first wharf surface from Lake Champlain, the accountability court heads to Rutland, and Mad River Glen more than doubles its footprint.

title: Friday, March 6th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Snow's Done, 50s Incoming, Gusts Up to 50 MPH – Church St Sales, Rhythm Arcades & Vermont's Snowiest Winter in 20 Years – Friday's mix clears out for a breezy, showery Saturday and a sunnier Sunday packed with sugar on snow, Irish singalongs, and 40+ women artisans. Inside: voters back the police and fire tax, the Prog veto holds, cheese makers go global, and a Malletts Bay hotel battle heats up.

title: Monday, March 9th
description: Monday's Spring Tease: Upper 50s & Gusty Winds, Ice Jam Alerts – VTANG in the Middle East, All the Rivers' Flynn Debut & Vermont's Population Slide – Warm temps won't last long as midweek rain and a Thursday snow flip loom. Tonight's packed City Council agenda tackles recycling contracts and equity appointments, while the week ahead brings seed swaps, Pecha Kucha, and hot dogs for a cause. Plus, Blue Cross bounces back from near collapse, the Bove's site plans expand, and Decker Towers has residents literally stuck.

title: Wednesday, March 11th
description: Brave Little State uncovers Vermont's volunteer firefighter crisis while Norwich bucks the trend. Taco Gordo wins the nachos debate at 40 upvotes, shelter pods in the Old North End go permanent, Burlington Square is 75% leased, and a student filmmaker desperately needs a pet toad. Town Meeting results flood the local podcast scene, a drive-thru lasagna dinner benefits Pathways Vermont, and pothole season has arrived with a vengeance. This weekend: Penguin Plunge into Lake Champlain, Pi Day pie on the farm in Shelburne, Before Dark Dance Party at SEABA, or a New England Hygge pop-up at Bar Renée. Then look ahead to STOMP at The Flynn, mini golf through the library stacks, and 90¢ wings at RJ's on Friday.

title: Friday, March 20th (I hope you didn't forget about me!)
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Afternoon Snow Burst, Maple Everything Saturday, NCAA Noon Tipoff – Taste the ONE Launches, 50+ Huskies Rescued & a Gun Bill Splits the Statehouse – Spring arrives with a slush-coated Friday commute before Saturday explodes with Maple Madness on Church Street, Ferment Fest on Pine, mini golf in the library stacks, and the Cats taking on Louisville. Plus, a new recovery shelter opens downtown, two Democrats are neck and neck for LG, and Vermont moves to let residents sue federal agents.

title: Monday, March 23rd
description: Your Monday Briefing: Black Ice & Snow Showers, City Council's ICE Showdown Tonight – Airport's Glassy New Terminal, City Market's Deepening Losses & Bear Season Arrives Early – Bundle up for a cloudy start as the council takes on the Dorset Street fallout, BTV preps a geothermal upgrade with mountain views, and Vermont's bears are already awake and hungry. Plus, Ilana Glazer's sold out, Lake Street Dive's drummer goes solo at Radio Bean, and the Flynn rolls out a Tony winner.

title: Wednesday, March 25th (Great updates!)
description: Brave Little State digs into Vermont's asbestos mine mystery while nobody knows if the fix will last. JP's Diner wins the pancakes debate at 13 upvotes, the Champlain Parkway is finally almost open after decades, and a South Burlington resident goes viral for grilling police on ICE collaboration. Free haircuts are back at Lux if you move fast, Maple Hill Barn in Johnson is worth the drive, and the food rut crowd is discovering Donwoori. This weekend: Maple Listening Party at Bar Renée Thursday, student plays and art at Rock Point Friday and Saturday. Then look ahead to Nick Viagas at the Light Club, pottery date night in Colchester, and $2 oysters at Hen of the Wood on Friday at 4. Plus, I launch my new webpage, The Btown Hub! And also my own outdoor Basketball League (BBL)!

title: Friday, March 27th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Frigid Friday, Chilly Saturday, Sunday's 40s Relief – No Kings Marches, Free Ben & Jerry's Fight & ICE Review Fallout – Bundle up for single-digit wind chills tonight before catching Cat Yoga, Tiki Weekend or a blacklight rave. Saturday's packed: three marches converge at City Hall Park, Grateful Drag hits Higher Ground, and the Farmers Market warms up Lumière Hall. Plus, the inside scoop on Ben Cohen's campaign to buy back his ice cream empire, the mayor's bodycam standoff

title: Monday, March 30th
description: Monday Warmth Before the Deluge: Highs Near 60, Then a Wet Week Ahead – A Burlington Officer Faces Charges, Speaking Volumes Gets a New Owner, and Two Weeks Later the ICE Raid Story Only Gets Stranger – Tonight: Diana Krall at the Flynn, Mike Gordon at Higher Ground, and a cozy craft night at Partizan. Plus, the Harborvale takes shape on Cherry Street and Frog & Toad's abrupt closure leaves families scrambling.

title: Wednesday, April 1st
description: Wednesday Edition: Vanity Fair, Blondie & Elijah Wood All Loved Her – Now Burlington's Heloise Williams Is Plotting a Comeback. A Local PT CEO Pulls Back the Curtain on Why Vermont Has the Highest Premiums in America, Reddit Has Spoken on the Best Burger, Rock Point Loop Hikes Are Back & the Spring Market Brings 60+ Artisans to Essex Junction

title: Friday, April 3rd
description: Your Easter Weekend Kickoff: Breezy 60s Today, Rain by Saturday Night, Cold Front Monday – Aly & AJ Sold Out, Phoebe Robinson's Four-Set Weekend, First Friday Everywhere – Tonight's gusts give way to a stacked lineup: catch $1 oysters at Gold, Clark Russell's new show on Pine Street, or free folk at Foam. Plus, egg hunts blanket the region Saturday, the labor board just torpedoed Scott's return-to-office mandate, Vermont's small colleges fight to survive, and a Burlington chef heads to the James

title: Monday, April 6th
description: Your Monday Briefing: Winter's Encore, Spring's Promise – State of the City Tonight, James Beard Finalists & Champlain Parkway's Finish Line – Bundle up for one last cold snap before sunny 50s return midweek. Inside: Burlington swears in new councilors, Fancy's heads to Chicago, Thingz From Yaad goes downtown, immigration fallout hits the Statehouse, and a packed week from Frozen at the Flynn to dragon boats on the lake.

title: Friday, April 10th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Wonderful Temps, Showers Later, and a Seriously Packed Burlington Weekend – Frozen at The Flynn, Sauna Fest, Spring Markets, Live Music & Plenty More to Get You Out the Door – Friday’s sunshine is a bit of a fake-out before clouds, light rain, and a cooler Saturday settle in, but the calendar is making a strong case for getting out anyway. From Frozen, Vermont Art Book Fair, and live music to lakeside saunas, farmers markets, dog-friendly outings, and coffee-fueled local po

title: Monday, April 13th
description: Your Monday Kickoff: Gusty Showers Today, 70s by the Weekend – City Council's Packed Agenda, Free Cone Day Tomorrow & Ben Cohen's Arrest – A breezy, wet start gives way to a warming trend all week, with Saturday shaping up as the gem. Tonight it's pie socials and cellar sales while Council tackles housing barriers and street policy. Plus, the fight over frozen anti-poverty funds, Noah Kahan is headed back to SNL, and a tiny house builder's YouTube empire.

title: Friday, April 17th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Foggy Friday, Sunny Saturday, Rainy Sunday Chill – Jazz Fest Lineup Drops, Rocky Horror Drama & Road Funding Crisis – Tonight's clearing skies set up a stacked Saturday: hit the last Winter Farmers Market, sample global flavors at the International Food Festival, or catch the Throwback Book Fair on Pine Street. Plus, a prosecutor's problem dog strikes again, Burlington's pod shelter hits a crossroads, and GMT eyes another bus cut – all before Sunday's cold front crashes the

title: Monday, April 20th
description: Your Monday Morning Briefing: Snow Showers & Black Ice Give Way to Midweek Spring – Devil Makes Three Tonight, Robin Pecknold Wednesday, Pattie Gonia Thursday – Bundle up for a record-cold April Monday before temps climb back to the 50s. This week's stacked: Earth Day EVs at Fletcher Free, a film fest in a historic Lumière building, Paint Night with adoptable cats, and the launch of Doña Esa's homestyle Mexican on Pine Street. Plus, UVM Medical Center's $460K daily losses, the Frog & Toad fallou

title: Friday, April 24th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Bright Skies, 60s & Monday Flirting With 70 – Sweetwaters Reopens, Body Cam Footage Drops & Denyce Graves Comes to BTV – Friday's stacked from The Flynn to Switchback to a 90s dance party, Saturday brings the Bike Swap, Bloom Market & Roller Disco. Plus, Sarah George declines ICE raid charges, a BBQ truck worth your lunch break, and Vermont's complicated relationship with fast food.

title: Monday, April 27th
description: 70° Monday Sunshine, Soaking Rain by Thursday – Festival of Fools Is Back, Union Votes Loom, a Demographic Cliff Sharpens – Soak up the warmest days of spring before the pattern flips, catch a silent reading session at Bar Renée tonight, and dig into why Healthy Living workers are organizing, Burlington's council is feuding over public safety, and the state's refugee pipeline has all but dried up.

title: Wednesday, April 28th
description: Midweek Roundup: Noah Kahan's Out of Body Doc – Cafe Hot's Breakfast Sandwich Reign, COTS Walk Sunday & Faster Trains to NYC – Go behind the scenes of the Netflix film with director Nick Sweeney, see which spots topped the r/burlington breakfast debate, and catch up on fiddlehead season, the missed connections boom, and Green Up Day this Saturday

title: Friday, May 1st (Green Up Weekend)
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Sunshine Today, Showers Tomorrow, Green Up Everything – Foam's 10th Birthday Bash, Vermont's Baby Bust & Two Union Votes on Dorset Street – Grab a flatbread and catch First Friday art walks before Saturday's rain meets Green Up Day cleanups across the county. Plus, why young Vermonters aren't having kids, a $20M bridge finally moving forward, and whether building more housing actually helps.

title: Monday, May 4th
description: Your Monday Briefing: Morning Showers, 70s Tuesday, Soaking Wednesday – VPA's $566K Settlement, Galvion's ICE Connection & a Senate Budget Battle – Sewing machines and Shakespeare kick off a packed Monday, Cinco de Mayo lights up Tuesday from Fiddlehead to Switchback, and the Moth GrandSLAM crowns a champion Wednesday at the Flynn. Plus, BTV's new terminal eyes record passengers, and a fourth-generation beekeeper bets on the future.

title: Friday, May 8th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Cloudy Friday, Warming Into the 60s for Mom – Café HOT.'s Bittersweet Goodbye, UVM Health's $300M Challenge & a New BHS Rising – Kick off tonight with flatbreads, dance parties and Laurie Kilmartin's dark humor, then hit Saturday's Farmers Market opener and free Shelburne Museum day before Sunday showers give way to Mother's Day sunshine.\

title: Monday, May 11th
description: Frosty Mornings, Warming Trends – Palmer Lane's parking wars, Sabah's House lands Church Street, Vermont eyes a paraquat ban & overdose deaths keep falling – Plus, King Tuff comes home, a new weather radar network, Shelburne's long game against the ash borer, and Wednesday's packed events lineup.

title: Friday, May 15th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Rain Clears Tonight, 70s All Weekend, 80s by Monday – South End Get Down Returns, Seussical, Glow 5K & a Stacked Saturday – Plus, the Mayor's $12M budget battle, ICE arrests in Williston, UVM's enrollment crunch, Grace Potter's GPN lineup drop, and a viral squirrel named Thompson

title: Tuesday, May 19th
description: 90° Heat & Storm Warnings, Ken Burns at the Flynn, A $100M South End Shakeup – Tuesday's sweltering temps give way to possible severe storms by evening, but the week ahead is packed: Church Street's Passeggiata returns, FEMA finally releases flood funds, and Burlington's biggest development deal in years just got the green light.

title: Wednesday, May 20th
description: Wednesday’s Worth-It Roundup: Ken Burns at the Flynn, Bike Bloom & a Downtown Mini Golf Hunt – Inside the Mayor’s Tax-Base Reckoning, El Cortijo’s Closing & Burlington’s Top Coffee – Catch the can’t-miss events before Sunday’s Marathon, then dig into UVM’s budget hole, City Council rejecting the Abenaki statue, and the roaster that beat them all.

title: Friday, May 22nd
description: Your Memorial Day Weekend Kickoff: Sunny Friday, Soggy Marathon Sunday – El Cortijo's Farewell, Quantum Jobs & Tax Crackdowns – Enjoy today's blue skies before rain crashes the marathon, then explore a brand new beer fest, Vermont Green GC games this weekend, free Revolutionary War reenactments and creemee season's grand opening. Plus, the Farmhouse Group's diner pivot, GlobalFoundries' $375M quantum bet, Burlington's aggressive push on overdue taxes, and why a raven family just hijacked a conce

title: Monday, May 25th (Memorial Day!)
description: Memorial Day Rain Clears for a Stunning Week Ahead – Waterfront Cleanups, Chapel Tours & City of the Sun Tonight – South Burlington's mail woes deepen, ticks reshape outdoor life, the Senate moves on an $83M homelessness overhaul, and Vermont quietly became a disc golf powerhouse. Plus, Lake Champlain's access problem is bigger than you think.

title: Friday, May 29th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Rainy Friday, Brighter Saturday, Mild Sunday – Trout Puppets Parade Downtown, Lake Monsters Open Their Season & Essex Pride Takes Over – Dodge the afternoon showers for the Burlington Trout Parade, then chase Saturday's sun toward Centennial Field and a packed slate of plant sales, 5ks, and country music. Plus the inside scoop on Krowinski's surprise exit and a childcare center's collapse.

title: Monday, June 1st
description: Your Week Ahead: Chilly Monday, Summer Warmth by Midweek – Jazz Takes Over Church Street, a Tree Parades Up Main & Hannaford Heads to the Table – Shake off the damp start, because sunshine and 80s are rolling in just as Discover Jazz fills downtown. Plus, a giant sculpture floats up Main Street, Migrant Justice scores a win, and Ben Cohen fights for the soul of Ben & Jerry's.

title: Friday, June 5th (Discover Jazz Festival!)
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Cloudy Friday, Stormy Saturday, Cooler Sunday – Jazz Fest's Big Finish, an ICE Force Ruling & City Market's Warning – Today's clouds give way to a rowdy Saturday before a cool, brighter Sunday: catch Mavis Staples free on the waterfront, race the Causeway, or skate to live jazz at Leddy. Plus the latest on cleared officers from the March 11 raid and the co-op's future. Plus, DJ KwameVibe sits down with DJ Cheetatah to talk music.

title: Monday, June 8th
description: Your Week Ahead: Sunny Start, Stormy Midweek – Greensky Bluegrass, Free State Parks Entry this Weekend & a One Night Heist Doc – Monday opens warm and bright before midweek showers roll in, so catch the Stolen Kingdom screening or lunchtime concerts in the park early. Plus, the inside scoop on UVM resident doctors fighting for a contract, the South End's thousand home housing bet, and free entry to every state park this weekend.

title: Friday, June 12th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Hot, Stormy Friday Before a Crisp, Sunny Weekend – Soccer Under the Stars, a $150K Police Settlement & Pizzeria Ida's Exit – Beat the heat with World Cup watch parties, Perrypalooza, and Saturday's packed farmers market, then dig into the cooling rent market, an AI smear targeting Rep. Balint, and Vermont's nation-leading maple haul.

title: Monday, June 15th
description: Your Monday Rundown: Rain Clears, Calendar Fills – Juneteenth Celebrations, a Class Says Farewell & Bears Make Headlines – Trade the wet morning for a bright stretch ahead, with Juneteenth events across Winooski and South Burlington, the last BHS class leaving the old Macy's, and the latest on UVM Health's cuts.

title: Friday, June 19th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Cloudy Friday, Showery Saturday, Sunny-er Sunday – World Cup Watch Parties, Juneteenth & a Nectar's Send-Off – Quieter skies finally roll in just as Btown fills up: catch the USA match, celebrate Juneteenth on Church Street, and toast a Burlington legend one last time. Plus the city budget passes, the Champlain Parkway gets an open date, and dads get their due all weekend long.

title: Monday, June 22nd
description: Your Week Ahead: Quick Monday Rain, Sunny Midweek Ahead – Solstice on Broadway, the Lake Monsters at BBCO & a Fire Show Thursday – A few showers slip past us to the south before Tuesday and Wednesday open up bright and warm. Toast the longest day with Broadway stars in South Hero, meet Champ over half-price hot dogs, then catch Cirque de Fuego close out a packed Thursday.

title: Friday, June 26th
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Stormy Friday Clearing to a Bright Sunday – ODD Fest, the Wine Festival & Renaissance Jousting – Wait out today's afternoon thunder for a weekend that turns golden: berry picking in Essex, hundreds of pours along the waterfront, and Pride marching through Battery Park. Plus the mayor says Burlington is back, BETA dreams big, and a fire lieutenant lands in court.

title: Monday, June 29th
description: Your Week Ahead: One Crisp Day Before Summer Really Cranks Up – Bikes to the Farm, Fancy Hot Dogs, Lunchtime Folk & a Ballpark Pickle Takeover – Make the most of sunny Monday before the humidity rolls in midweek, with a CSA group ride, lakefront cleanups making headlines, a needle exchange changing hands, and a downtown packed with concerts, BBQs, 5Ks, and a symphony sendoff to the Fourth.

title: Friday, July 3rd (America's 250th!)
description: Your Weekend Kickoff: Hot Friday, Muggy Fourth, Comfortable Sunday – Fireworks, Farmers Markets & A Historic Playoff Night – Chase the year's biggest fireworks show at the waterfront, First Friday art on Pine Street, and cheer Vermont Green FC's first-ever playoff match, plus the inside scoop on Burlington's long-awaited new parkway and the AC debate heating up across Vermont.

title: Saturday, July 11th
description: Your Weekend Rundown: Wall-to-Wall Sun, Hot Monday – The BTown Digital Arcade Opens, Green FC's National Semifinal & Mom Prom on the Lake – Play 18 brand new local games and explore a massive City Guide expansion, catch the undefeated Green FC women at Virtue Field Saturday, and join our cheese potluck picnic at Oakledge, plus the 61-year Champlain Parkway saga and Burlington's 15 applicants for police chief.

title: Monday, July 13th
description: Your Week Ahead: Warm Monday, Near-100 Tuesday, Gorgeous Wednesday – Green FC Women Reach the National Final, Jack White at the Expo & Bastille Day with The Moth – The undefeated Women in Green punch their ticket to Seattle, Lomelda plays Radio Bean tonight, Winooski throws a block party, and Kurosawa hits the big screen Thursday, plus shelter accountability questions, Kurrle's exit to Vermont Gas and Beta's hiring boom.

title: Friday, July 17th
description: The Smoke Clears for a Gorgeous Friday – Block Party Downtown, Brewers Fest at 5:30 & Brownell Library Turns 100 Tonight – Steamroller Printmaking and Summervale Saturday – Argentina vs Spain Sunday at 3 With Free Watch Parties, Lake Monsters Under the Lights & Ben & Jerry's Foundation News

title: Monday, July 20th
description: Sunshine Now, Storms Midweek, Perfect Thursday – Gillian Welch & David Rawlings Tuesday, Sam Mazza's Free Concert, Loon Chicks on the Lake & Molly Kearney at VCC – Plus Vermont's 235th Birthday, the Sheriff Race Like No Other & Young Vermonters Eyeing the Exits
